name: standing-stones-circle
summary: A ring of upright stones on a low rise or moor. Older than any living people. No structure, no shelter — just stone, grass, sky, and a quiet that feels attentive.

accent.purpose: trampled ritual center — flattened grass or bare earth

primary_palette.note: full ring — 6-9 stones evenly spaced around an open center
altar-stone at the center of the ring, stones around the perimeter
taller center stone — axis mundi surrounded by lesser stones
incomplete ring — one stone toppled across the line, gap in the circle

story_prompts: silent and neutral — just old stones, but every animal avoids the ring
active ley-line — the stones hum faintly, spells cast within are amplified
mid-ritual — hooded figures, torches, something is being invoked tonight
damaged — someone toppled a stone; the broken ring is leaking something
the stones move when no one is watching — last visitor's notes mapped a different layout

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: open-sky — bright by day, starlit by night, no fixed light sources

anti_patterns: no built walls — the stones are the landmark, the moor is the floor
do not over-prop the center — an altar or bare ground, nothing more
stones should be evenly spaced unless the story calls for a fallen one — don't clump them
no buildings, no tents — this is a place people visit and leave
